purpose: Claude Code agent definition for `swarm-worker`. It configures the agent role, loaded skills, and behavioral constraints used by the swarm runtime. Key content: --- name: swarm-worker description: > Synapse swarm worker agent. Implements a single task from a parallel swarm and reports progress via progress files. Use when dispatched by a master orchestrator to execute a specific task with live dashboard tracking. skills: hooks: PostToolUse: hooks: command: ".claude/hooks/validate-progress-file.sh" --- The file is 69 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
patterns: Control-plane documentation pattern: markdown is treated as operational configuration for agents, not just explanatory prose.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
Agent-control documents use `{tracker_root}`, `{project_root}`, and `{dashboardId}` placeholders instead of machine-specific paths.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
